rulings.Case Title: IRENE BOLLINGER VS. CALAVERAS ASBESTOS LTD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 28, 2016 in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant Calaveras Asbestos, Ltd.'s motion for summary judgment or in the alternative summary adjudication is denied as untimely. Defendant failed to provide proper notice required by C.C.P. §§ 437c(a), 1010.6(a)(4) and CRC 2.251(h). Defendant's proof of service demonstrates it electronically served its motion November 13, 2015. With a January 28, 2016 hearing date, that is insufficient notice. Plaintiffs' objection based on timeliness is sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: LINDA PONCHIONE VS. CONSOLIDATED INSULATION, INC.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 28, 2016 in Department 503, Line 4. Defendant STM Automotive, Inc.'s demurrer to plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint pursuant to C.C.P. §§ 430.10(e) and 340.2 is overruled in its entirety. Defendant's special demurrer to plaintiffs' third (negligence I - wrongful death) and fourth (products liability - wrongful death) causes of action is overruled. Although plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint was filed beyond the asbestos wrongful death limitations period enunciated in C.C.P. § 340.2(c), the third and fourth causes of action are not time barred because they relate back to the timely filing of plaintiffs' initial complaint. Further, the First Amended Complaint states facts sufficient to demonstrate that decedent's death was caused by his exposure to asbestos-containing products and materials and subsequent mesothelioma and that his suicide was not an independent intervening cause, an argument that defendant did not explicitly make in its demurrer. Defendant's demurrer to the sixth cause of action (negligence II - wrongful death) is overruled as moot as that cause of action is not alleged against defendant. (See defendant's Exhibit B at p. 29:3-10 and Exhibit C at p. 1:23-24.) Defendant has 10 days to answer. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: LINDA PONCHIONE VS. CONSOLIDATED INSULATION, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, January 28, 2016 in Department 503, Line 4. Defendant STM Automotive, Inc.'s motion to strike portions of plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint pursuant to C.C.P. §§ 435 and 436 is denied in its entirety. As to the motion to strike the third (negligence I - wrongful death) and fourth (products liability - wrongful death) causes of action, a motion to strike an entire cause of action is improper. (See Quiroz v. Seventh Avenue Center (2006) 140 Cal.App.4th 1256, 1281; Ferraro v. Camarlinghi (2008) 161 Cal.App.4th 509, 528.) Defendant's motion to strike the sixth cause of action (negligence II - wrongful death) is denied as moot as that cause of action is not alleged against defendant. As to defendant's motion to strike items nos. 1, 3, 4 and 5 from plaintiffs' request for damages, pursuant to this Court's ruling on defendant's demurrer, valid causes of action are stated in the First Amended Complaint and defendant failed to demonstrate that the challenged damages are not supported by the causes of action pleaded. Defendant has 10 days to answer. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)
